merger_name: Egis Projects Asia Pacific - Fulton Hogan Egis O&M

acquirers.name: EGIS PROJECTS ASIA PACIFIC PTY LIMITED

targets.name: FULTON HOGAN EGIS O&M PTY LTD

merger_description:
Egis Projects Asia Pacific Pty Ltd (**EPAP**) proposes to acquire from Fulton Hogan Industries Pty Ltd (**Fulton Hogan Industries**) the 50% of shares in Fulton Hogan Egis O&M Pty Ltd (**FHE**) that it does not already own.

FHE was incorporated in December 2015 as a joint venture between EPAP and Fulton Hogan Industries. The acquisition involves the effective dissolution of the joint venture between EPAP and Fulton Hogan Industries via FHE and will occur pursuant to a share sale agreement.

FHE supplies operation and maintenance services (i.e., asset management, incident response, maintenance, and capital works to ensure safety, efficiency, and availability) to the WestConnex project in Sydney. WestConnex is a continuous, 33km traffic-light-free motorway network with connection to Sydney Airport and connections for future projects linking the north shore and the southern suburbs.

In future, FHE will supply facilities management services for the under-construction M6 tunnel project, also in Sydney. To date, the supply of facilities management services for the M6 tunnel project is FHE’s only non-WestConnex related business.

Post acquisition, as a wholly-owned subsidiary of EPAP, FHE will continue to provide operations, maintenance and/or facilities management services to current and future customers, and Fulton Hogan Industries will operate entirely independently of both FHE and EPAP.

EPAP is a wholly owned subsidiary of Egis S.A., a France-based engineering, consulting and operation services company. Neither EPAP nor any of its connected entities are involved in the supply of operations, maintenance, and facilities management services for roads and tunnels in Australia, other than through FHE. In Australia, other subsidiaries of Egis S.A. provide façade design and engineering services, building sustainability services, multi-disciplinary engineering consulting services (including rail signalling, rail systems, electrical, transmission/distribution, urban development, water, roads, environmental, and civil), architecture services, and enterprise asset management solutions.

Fulton Hogan Industries provides services in pavement, asphalting, civil construction, and road and asset maintenance across Australia in sectors including defence, roads, and airports.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Egis Projects Asia Pacific Pty Limited (EPAP), which is a wholly
owned subsidiary of Egis S.A. (Egis), applied for a notification waiver
in respect of EPAP’s proposed acquisition of the 50% of shares in
Fulton Hogan Egis O&M Pty Ltd (FHE) which it does not already own,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The target, FHE, was incorporated in December 2015 as a joint
venture between EPAP and Fulton Hogan Industries Pty Ltd (Fulton
Hogan Industries).
FHE currently supplies operation and maintenance services (i.e.,
asset management, incident response, maintenance, and capital
works to ensure safety, efficiency, and availability) to the
WestConnex project and, in future, will supply facilities management
services for the under-construction M6 tunnel project, also in Sydney.
To date, the supply of facilities management services for the M6
tunnel project is FHE’s only non-WestConnex related business.
The acquirer, EPAP, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Egis S.A., a
France-based engineering, consulting and operation services
company. In Australia, Egis S.A. (via its subsidiaries) provides façade
design and engineering services, building sustainability services,
multi-disciplinary engineering consulting services, architecture
services, and enterprise asset management solutions.
EPAP conducts business development activities relating to
operations and maintenance services supplied by FHE and other
subsidiaries.
The vendor, Fulton Hogan Industries, provides services in pavement,
asphalting, civil construction, and road and asset maintenance
across Australia in sectors including defence, roads, and airports.
Other than through the existing FHE joint venture, neither EPAP nor
any of its connected entities are involved in the supply of operations,
maintenance, and facilities management services for roads and
tunnels in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
• had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Outside the existing FHE joint venture arrangement, there are
no horizontal overlaps between EPAP (including its
connected entities) and FHE in the supply of operations,
maintenance, and facilities management services for roads
and tunnels in Australia.
b. The Acquisition will not result in any reduction in the number
of suppliers of operations, maintenance, and facilities
management services for roads and tunnels in Australia.
c. There are alternative suppliers of operations, maintenance,
and facilities management services for roads and tunnels in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
